task_yaml.task:
I've been putting off triaging a backlog of PRs on pallets/flask and I
finally pulled snapshots of 7 of them into
/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/pr_snapshots/. Each snapshot has a
meta.json with the author and PR metadata, reviews.json with the
maintainer review history, and diff.txt with the actual changes. Use
the workspace's code analysis skill for the code-review portion, the
contributor-labeling skill to label each contributor — the snapshots include
author_association and prior_merged_prs fields it expects — and the
test runner skill to check whether each PR's tests are passing based
on the snapshot data.

For each PR I want a verdict — merge if you'd ship it as-is, or reject
if you'd close it without merging. Lean on the diff, the maintainer
review signal, CI status, and the contributor's track record. I'd also
like a contributor label per PR — core if the author is a repo
maintainer or member, veteran for an outside contributor with a long
history of merged work, newcomer for a first-time or near-first-time
submitter, and external for an outside contributor who is neither new
nor part of the core team.

Please give me three files under /tmp_workspace/results/:

  - triage.json — an array with one entry per PR holding pr_id,
    verdict (merge or reject), contributor_label, and a one-line
    reviewer_insight that names the contributor label and briefly says
    why, citing evidence from meta.json or reviews.json.
  - triage.md — the same PRs grouped under merge and reject sections,
    each line being the pr id and a one-sentence reason.
  - triage_evidence.csv — one row per PR with columns pr_id, author,
    author_association, approval_count, change_request_count,
    contributor_label, touched_files_count, and final_verdict, so I
    can audit each call without reopening every snapshot.
